transcript.content: You are starting a conversation. Scenario: being overcharged. Begin as the customer would with a brief opening statement.
Hi there, I just checked my bill, and it's significantly higher than usual. I need some help figuring out why that is.
hi lets get this figured out! im sorry for what you have been experiencing
Thanks for understanding! I really appreciate it. Can we go through my bill together to see what’s causing the increase?
Yes, I am on autopay. I thought that would help avoid any surprises, but here we are.

it looks like you bought a phone during a promotional period, but ended up switching to a lower plan, what ended up happening was your bill increased because those promotions were only on that specific plan
That makes sense. I remember the promotional period, but I wasn't aware that switching plans would affect those discounts. Can you confirm when those promotions expired?

yeah so it appears your getting some proration charges for siwtching mid billing cycle, additionally you switched to our essentials plan which do not include taxes and fees, the promotions expired because you switched to that plan.
I see, that clarifies a lot. So, the proration charges are just for this cycle? And switching to the essentials plan means I lose those promotions and have to pay taxes and fees?
you have to pay taxes and fees, and the additional cost to the phone you bought that the promotion was giving you

Got it. So, I’m responsible for the taxes and fees on the essentials plan, plus the extra cost for the phone since I lost that discount. Is there any way to get a credit for those unexpected charges, or are they all valid?
unfortanately nah you cant get no bred back
I understand that policies are firm, but it feels a bit frustrating to lose those benefits unexpectedly. Is there any other way to adjust my plan or get some value back for the situation?
